Database: SQL Server (T-SQL). Schema dw.
Tables: dw.draft, dw.games, dw.teams, dw.venues, dw.school_type_lookup

dw.draft columns: year, pick_number, pick_round, person__full_name,
  person__primary_position__name, home__state, home__city, team__name,
  school__name, signing_bonus (nvarchar - CAST to decimal for math)

dw.games columns: game_pk, game_date, series_description, double_header,
  status__detailed_state, teams__home__score, teams__away__score,
  teams__home__team__id, teams__away__team__id, teams__home__is_winner

dw.teams columns: id, name, team_name, abbreviation, league__name, division__name

dw.school_type_lookup columns: school_name, school_type (High School/Junior College/University/College)

RULES:
- Use TOP (N) not LIMIT
- Use LIKE not ILIKE
- Use YEAR() not EXTRACT()
- Use CAST() not :: operator
- Never use NULLS FIRST/LAST
- dw.draft.signing_bonus is nvarchar — always CAST(signing_bonus AS decimal(18,2))
- State filter: use IN ('CA', 'California') not = 'CA'
input: name, position of draft picks whose home state is California or CA in 2025 draft
output: SELECT TOP (100) d.person__full_name, d.person__primary_position__name FROM dw.draft d WHERE d.year = 2025 AND d.home__state IN ('CA', 'California') ORDER BY d.pick_number;

input: how many games did the San Francisco Giants play the Los Angeles Dodgers in 2025
output: SELECT COUNT(*) AS game_count FROM dw.games g JOIN dw.teams t_home ON g.teams__home__team__id = t_home.id JOIN dw.teams t_away ON g.teams__away__team__id = t_away.id WHERE ((t_home.name = 'San Francisco Giants' AND t_away.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers') OR (t_home.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers' AND t_away.name = 'San Francisco Giants')) AND YEAR(g.game_date) = 2025 AND g.status__detailed_state = 'Final';

dw.school_type_lookup columns: school_name, school_type (High School/Junior College/University/College)

RULES:
- Use TOP (N) not LIMIT

input: game_pk, winner, game date how many games did the San Francisco Giants play the Los Angeles Dodgers in 2025
output: SELECT TOP (100) g.game_pk, CASE WHEN g.teams__home__score > g.teams__away__score THEN t_home.name ELSE t_away.name END AS winner, g.game_date FROM dw.games g JOIN dw.teams t_home ON g.teams__home__team__id = t_home.id JOIN dw.teams t_away ON g.teams__away__team__id = t_away.id WHERE ((t_home.name = 'San Francisco Giants' AND t_away.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers') OR (t_home.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers' AND t_away.name = 'San Francisco Giants')) AND YEAR(g.game_date) = 2025 AND g.status__detailed_state = 'Final' ORDER BY g.game_date;

Database: SQL Server (T-SQL). Schema dw.
Tables: dw.draft, dw.games, dw.teams, dw.venues, dw.school_type_lookup

dw.draft columns: year, pick_number, pick_round, person__full_name,
  person__primary_position__name, home__state, home__city, team__name,
  school__name, signing_bonus (nvarchar - CAST to decimal for math)

dw.games columns: game_pk, game_date, series_description, double_header,
  status__detailed_state, teams__home__score, teams__away__score,
  teams__home__team__id, teams__away__team__id, teams__home__is_winner

dw.teams columns: id, name, team_name, abbreviation, league__name, division__name

dw.school_type_lookup columns: school_name, school_type (High School/Junior College/University/College)

RULES:
- Use TOP (N) not LIMIT
- Use LIKE not ILIKE
- Use YEAR() not EXTRACT()
- Use CAST() not :: operator
- Never use NULLS FIRST/LAST
- dw.draft.signing_bonus is nvarchar — always CAST(signing_bonus AS decimal(18,2))
- State filter: use IN ('CA', 'California') not = 'CA'
input: game_pk, winner or loser, game date for all double headers in the National League in 2025
output: SELECT TOP (100) g.game_pk, t_home.name AS home_team_name, t_away.name AS away_team_name, g.teams__home__score, g.teams__away__score, CASE WHEN g.teams__home__score > g.teams__away__score THEN t_home.name WHEN g.teams__away__score > g.teams__home__score THEN t_away.name ELSE 'Tie' END AS winner, CASE WHEN g.teams__home__score < g.teams__away__score THEN t_home.name WHEN g.teams__away__score < g.teams__home__score THEN t_away.name ELSE 'Tie' END AS loser, g.game_date, g.double_header FROM dw.games g JOIN dw.teams t_home ON g.teams__home__team__id = t_home.id JOIN dw.teams t_away ON g.teams__away__team__id = t_away.id WHERE YEAR(g.game_date) = 2025 AND g.double_header = 'Y' AND g.status__detailed_state = 'Final' AND (t_home.league__name = 'National League' OR t_away.league__name = 'National League') ORDER BY g.game_date, g.game_pk;

Database: SQL Server (T-SQL). Schema dw.
Tables: dw.draft, dw.games, dw.teams, dw.venues, dw.school_type_lookup

dw.draft columns: year, pick_number, pick_round, person__full_name,
  person__primary_position__name, home__state, home__city, team__name,

dw.games columns: game_pk, game_date, series_description, double_header,
  status__detailed_state, teams__home__score, teams__away__score,
  teams__home__team__id, teams__away__team__id, teams__home__is_winner

dw.teams columns: id, name, team_name, abbreviation, league__name, division__name

dw.school_type_lookup columns: school_name, school_type (High School/Junior College/University/College)

RULES:
- Use TOP (N) not LIMIT

- Use YEAR() not EXTRACT()

- State filter: use IN ('CA', 'California') not = 'CA'
input: game_pk, opponent, winner or loser, score and game date how many regular season games did the San Francisco Giants play National League West opponents in 2025 and what were the results
output: SELECT TOP (100) g.game_pk, t_home.name AS home_team, t_away.name AS away_team, CASE WHEN t_home.name = 'San Francisco Giants' THEN 'Home' ELSE 'Away' END AS giants_home_or_away, CASE WHEN t_home.name = 'San Francisco Giants' AND g.teams__home__score > g.teams__away__score THEN 'Winner' WHEN t_home.name = 'San Francisco Giants' AND g.teams__home__score < g.teams__away__score THEN 'Loser' WHEN t_away.name = 'San Francisco Giants' AND g.teams__away__score > g.teams__home__score THEN 'Winner' WHEN t_away.name = 'San Francisco Giants' AND g.teams__away__score < g.teams__home__score THEN 'Loser' ELSE 'Tie' END AS giants_result, g.teams__home__score AS home_score, g.teams__away__score AS away_score, g.game_date FROM dw.games g JOIN dw.teams t_home ON g.teams__home__team__id = t_home.id JOIN dw.teams t_away ON g.teams__away__team__id = t_away.id WHERE YEAR(g.game_date) = 2025 AND g.series_description = 'Regular Season' AND g.status__detailed_state = 'Final' AND (t_home.name = 'San Francisco Giants' OR t_away.name = 'San Francisco Giants') AND t_home.division__name = 'National League West' AND t_away.division__name = 'National League West' ORDER BY g.game_date;

input: pitcher, pitcher hand, batter side, pitch type, for strikeout pitches total strikeouts called or swinging and total strikeouts over 20
output: SELECT pitcher_name, pitcher_hand, batter_side, pitch_type, total_strikeouts, called_k, swinging_k FROM (SELECT p.pitcher_name, p.pitcher_hand, p.batter_side, p.pitch_type, COUNT(*) AS total_strikeouts, SUM(CASE WHEN p.is_called_strike = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS called_k, SUM(CASE WHEN p.is_whiff = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS swinging_k, AVG(p.start_speed) AS avg_velo, AVG(p.spin_rate) AS avg_spin, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.pitcher_name, p.pitcher_hand, p.batter_side ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rn FROM dw.pitch_core p WHERE p.strikes = 2 AND (p.is_called_strike = 1 OR p.is_whiff = 1) AND p.season = 2025 GROUP BY p.pitcher_name, p.pitcher_hand, p.batter_side, p.pitch_type HAVING COUNT(*) > 20) ranked WHERE rn = 1 ORDER BY pitcher_name, batter_side;

input: average exit velocity and barrel rate by season 2020 to 2025

Database: SQL Server (T-SQL). Schema dw.
Tables: dw.draft, dw.games, dw.teams, dw.venues, dw.school_type_lookup

dw.draft columns: year, pick_number, pick_round, person__full_name,
  person__primary_position__name, home__state, home__city, team__name,
  school__name, signing_bonus (nvarchar - CAST to decimal for math)

dw.games columns: game_pk, game_date, series_description, double_header,
  status__detailed_state, teams__home__score, teams__away__score,
  teams__home__team__id, teams__away__team__id, teams__home__is_winner

dw.teams columns: id, name, team_name, abbreviation, league__name, division__name

dw.school_type_lookup columns: school_name, school_type (High School/Junior College/University/College)

RULES:
- Use TOP (N) not LIMIT
- Use LIKE not ILIKE
- Use YEAR() not EXTRACT()
- Use CAST() not :: operator
- Never use NULLS FIRST/LAST
- dw.draft.signing_bonus is nvarchar — always CAST(signing_bonus AS decimal(18,2))
- State filter: use IN ('CA', 'California') not = 'CA'
input: top 20 slugging percentage leaders in 2025
output: SELECT TOP (20) ps.player__full_name, ps.team__name, ps.season, ps.stat__slg, ps.stat__total_bases, ps.stat__home_runs, ps.stat__at_bats FROM dw.player_stats ps WHERE ps.season = 2025 AND ps.stat__slg IS NOT NULL AND ps.stat__plate_appearances >= 300 ORDER BY CAST(ps.stat__slg AS decimal(10,3)) DESC;

input: league hitting stats averages in 2025
output: SELECT ps.league__name, ps.season, AVG(CAST(ps.stat__avg AS decimal(10,3))) AS league_avg, AVG(CAST(ps.stat__obp AS decimal(10,3))) AS league_obp, AVG(CAST(ps.stat__slg AS decimal(10,3))) AS league_slg, AVG(CAST(ps.stat__ops AS decimal(10,3))) AS league_ops, SUM(ps.stat__home_runs) AS total_hr FROM dw.player_stats ps WHERE ps.season = 2025 AND ps.stat__avg IS NOT NULL AND ps.stat__plate_appearances >= 100 AND ps.position__type = 'Hitter' GROUP BY ps.league__name, ps.season ORDER BY ps.league__name;

dw.games columns: game_pk, game_date, series_description, double_header,
  status__detailed_state, teams__home__score, teams__away__score,

dw.teams columns: id, name, team_name, abbreviation, league__name, division__name

dw.school_type_lookup columns: school_name, school_type (High School/Junior College/University/College)

input: win loss record for each team in 2025 regular season
output: SELECT team, SUM(wins) AS wins, SUM(losses) AS losses, CAST(SUM(wins) AS float) / NULLIF(SUM(wins) + SUM(losses), 0) AS win_pct FROM (SELECT teams__home__team__name AS team, CASE WHEN teams__home__is_winner = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS wins, CASE WHEN teams__home__is_winner = 0 AND is_tie = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS losses FROM dw.games WHERE season = '2025' AND series_description = 'Regular Season' AND status__detailed_state = 'Final' UNION ALL SELECT teams__away__team__name AS team, CASE WHEN teams__away__is_winner = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS wins, CASE WHEN teams__away__is_winner = 0 AND is_tie = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS losses FROM dw.games WHERE season = '2025' AND series_description = 'Regular Season' AND status__detailed_state = 'Final') r GROUP BY team ORDER BY win_pct DESC;

input: all 2025 regular season games for a specific team New York Yankees including opponent, score, home or away, and date
output: SELECT TOP (162) game_pk, CASE WHEN teams__home__team__name = 'New York Yankees' THEN 'Home' ELSE 'Away' END AS home_away, CASE WHEN teams__home__team__name = 'New York Yankees' THEN teams__away__team__name ELSE teams__home__team__name END AS opponent, teams__home__score AS home_score, teams__away__score AS away_score, CASE WHEN teams__home__team__name = 'New York Yankees' AND teams__home__is_winner = 1 THEN 'W' WHEN teams__away__team__name = 'New York Yankees' AND teams__away__is_winner = 1 THEN 'W' WHEN is_tie = 1 THEN 'T' ELSE 'L' END AS result, game_date FROM dw.games WHERE season = '2025' AND series_description = 'Regular Season' AND status__detailed_state = 'Final' AND (teams__home__team__name = 'New York Yankees' OR teams__away__team__name = 'New York Yankees') ORDER BY game_date;

input: all 2025 regular season games for the Los Angeles Dodgers including opponent score and result
output: SELECT TOP (162) g.game_pk, CASE WHEN t_home.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers' THEN 'Home' ELSE 'Away' END AS home_away, CASE WHEN t_home.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers' THEN t_away.name ELSE t_home.name END AS opponent, g.teams__home__score AS home_score, g.teams__away__score AS away_score, CASE WHEN t_home.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers' AND g.teams__home__is_winner = 1 THEN 'W' WHEN t_away.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers' AND g.teams__away__is_winner = 1 THEN 'W' WHEN g.is_tie = 1 THEN 'T' ELSE 'L' END AS result, g.game_date FROM dw.games g JOIN dw.teams t_home ON g.teams__home__team__id = t_home.id JOIN dw.teams t_away ON g.teams__away__team__id = t_away.id WHERE YEAR(g.game_date) = 2025 AND g.series_description = 'Regular Season' AND g.status__detailed_state = 'Final' AND (t_home.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers' OR t_away.name = 'Los Angeles Dodgers') ORDER BY g.game_date;

input: win loss record for each American League team in 2025 regular season
output: SELECT team, SUM(wins) AS wins, SUM(losses) AS losses, CAST(SUM(wins) AS float) / NULLIF(SUM(wins) + SUM(losses), 0) AS win_pct FROM (SELECT t_home.name AS team, CASE WHEN g.teams__home__is_winner = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS wins, CASE WHEN g.teams__home__is_winner = 0 AND g.is_tie = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS losses FROM dw.games g JOIN dw.teams t_home ON g.teams__home__team__id = t_home.id WHERE g.season = '2025' AND g.series_description = 'Regular Season' AND g.status__detailed_state = 'Final' AND t_home.league__name = 'American League' UNION ALL SELECT t_away.name AS team, CASE WHEN g.teams__away__is_winner = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS wins, CASE WHEN g.teams__away__is_winner = 0 AND g.is_tie = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS losses FROM dw.games g JOIN dw.teams t_away ON g.teams__away__team__id = t_away.id WHERE g.season = '2025' AND g.series_description = 'Regular Season' AND g.status__detailed_state = 'Final' AND t_away.league__name = 'American League') r GROUP BY team ORDER BY win_pct DESC;

dw.draft columns: year, pick_number, pick_round, person__full_name,
  person__primary_position__name, home__state, home__city, team__name,
  school__name, signing_bonus (nvarchar - CAST to decimal for math)

dw.games columns: game_pk, game_date, series_description, double_header,
  status__detailed_state, teams__home__score, teams__away__score,
  teams__home__team__id, teams__away__team__id, teams__home__is_winner

dw.teams columns: id, name, team_name, abbreviation, league__name, division__name

dw.school_type_lookup columns: school_name, school_type (High School/Junior College/University/College)

RULES:
- Use TOP (N) not LIMIT
- Use LIKE not ILIKE
- Use YEAR() not EXTRACT()
- Use CAST() not :: operator
- Never use NULLS FIRST/LAST
- dw.draft.signing_bonus is nvarchar — always CAST(signing_bonus AS decimal(18,2))
- State filter: use IN ('CA', 'California') not = 'CA'
input: all active players born in Panama
output: SELECT TOP (100) p.full_name, p.birth_city, p.birth_country, p.primary_position__name, p.height, p.weight, p.mlb_debut_date FROM dw.mlbplayers p WHERE p.active = 1 AND p.birth_country = 'Panama' ORDER BY p.full_name;

input: pitcher batter side and pitch type for most used pitches on three ball counts in 2025
output: SELECT pitcher_name, pitcher_hand, batter_side, pitch_type FROM (SELECT p.pitcher_name, p.pitcher_hand, p.batter_side, p.pitch_type, COUNT(*) AS total_pitches, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.pitcher_name, p.batter_side ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rn FROM dw.pitch_core p WHERE p.balls = 3 AND p.season = 2025 GROUP BY p.pitcher_name, p.pitcher_hand, p.batter_side, p.pitch_type HAVING COUNT(*) > 20) ranked WHERE rn = 1 ORDER BY pitcher_name, batter_side;
